rationale.R1.1: Any substantive 'what do you think' response on a research artifact requires reconstructing the thesis (panel-of-models filtering improves hit rate), gap statement (single-model limits), and contribution claim (plug-and-play post-hoc filter); needed as foundation regardless of which direction the critique takes.
rationale.R1.2: The paper chains several logical steps — panel diversity → uncorrelated errors → stronger consensus → hit-rate enrichment — that need consistency checking; this is a plausible primary target of the delegation.
rationale.R1.3: Central empirical claim (2.9–3.4× enrichment, 30% ablation drop, kinase case study) requires checking whether stated evidence (ChEMBL retrospective screen, docking results) actually supports those numbers; equally plausible primary target.
rationale.R1.4: Panel/ensemble filtering for generative drug discovery is a crowded space; novelty delta vs. prior ensemble screening work (e.g., ADMET consensus scoring, multi-objective RL) is a plausible primary target of 'what do you think'.
rationale.R1.7: ChEMBL citation and prior ensemble work attribution are peripherally checkable but not the obvious focus without more specific direction.
